pi_items.source: setup_task.sh creates file via heredoc at line 26 and chowns at line 69

Data file (generated in setup_task.sh) specifies 9 unique nodes across three loops: Global Mean Temperature, Arctic Sea Ice Extent, Planetary Albedo, Solar Energy Absorption, Permafrost Thaw Rate, Atmospheric CO2 & Methane, Greenhouse Effect, Ocean Evaporation, Atmospheric Water Vapor

Data file specifies 3 reinforcing feedback loops with 4 edges each (12 total): Loop 1 (Ice-Albedo): Tempâ†’Ice, Iceâ†’Albedo, Albedoâ†’Absorption, Absorptionâ†’Temp. Loop 2 (Permafrost): Tempâ†’Permafrost, Permafrostâ†’CO2, CO2â†’Greenhouse, Greenhouseâ†’Temp. Loop 3 (Water Vapor): Tempâ†’Evaporation, Evaporationâ†’Vapor, Vaporâ†’Greenhouse, Greenhouseâ†’Temp
Data file (setup_task.sh lines 33, 43, 53) explicitly defines: 'ICE-ALBEDO FEEDBACK', 'PERMAFROST CARBON FEEDBACK', 'WATER VAPOR FEEDBACK'

privileged_info_summary: The task is a causal loop diagram creation exercise using draw.io. The data is completely synthetic, generated by setup_task.sh. The feedback loop data specifies three reinforcing loops: (1) Ice-Albedo loop with Temperatureâ†’Arctic Sea Ice Extentâ†’Planetary Albedoâ†’Solar Energy Absorptionâ†’Temperature; (2) Permafrost loop with Temperatureâ†’Permafrost Thaw Rateâ†’Atmospheric CO2 & Methaneâ†’Greenhouse Effectâ†’Temperature; (3) Water Vapor loop with Temperatureâ†’Ocean Evaporationâ†’Atmospheric Water Vaporâ†’Greenhouse Effectâ†’Temperature. The task requires creating a diagram with all three loops, polarity labels (+/-), and 'R' labels for reinforcing loops. File outputs are climate_loops.drawio (draw.io format) and climate_loops.png (PNG export). The metadata claims min_nodes=6 and min_edges=6, but the actual data structure requires 9 unique nodes and 12 directed edges total.
